tier: A5 / A-e: APFS transparent compression (com.apple.decmpfs) read + write
host: Host pre-check: apfsprogs apfsck (Corellium/Ernesto Fernandez, built in WSL Arch) run with -cw. The S.A.K. APFS writer CLI (sak_apfs_writer_cli.exe) produced the compressed container; macOS-kernel mount + native-Apple fsck_apfs + md5 byte-match is the certifying confirmation.
change: Implemented the A-e driver core for inline zlib compression: (1) WRITE -- commit-image/raw-file-insert take --compress-zlib; the file is stored transparently compressed in an embedded `com.apple.decmpfs` extended attribute (16-byte apfs_compress_hdr {signature='cmpf', algo=APFS_COMPRESS_ZLIB_ATTR=3, uncompressed_size} + a standard zlib stream, or a 0xFF-prefixed stored block when compression does not shrink the data), with NO data stream; the inode is stamped UF_COMPRESSED (bsd_flags 0x20) and carries uncompressed_size paired with APFS_INODE_HAS_UNCOMPRESSED_SIZE (internal_flags 0x40000). It runs on the certified crash-safe in-place COW checkpoint engine (0 data blocks, 0 extent-ref slots -- a metadata-only insert). New shared header include/sak/apfs_compression.h holds the decmpfs constants + inline zlib codec (reused by writer and reader). (2) READ -- the APFS reader enumerates xattr records (j_xattr_key/val), captures an embedded com.apple.decmpfs attribute, and on read decodes it (inline zlib/plain) back to the original bytes instead of reading the absent data stream; directory listings report the decmpfs uncompressed_size. Layout/constraints mirror Apple apfs/raw.h and apfsck compress.c/xattr.c (the validator the cert satisfies): apfsck's parse_decmpfs fail-closes with 'is not compressed but has decmpfs xattr' unless the inode is correctly UF_COMPRESSED, and apfs_compress_open inflates the inline zlib to exactly hdr.size. Larger files whose compressed attribute would exceed APFS_XATTR_MAX_EMBEDDED_SIZE (3804) fail closed (resource-fork compression is a documented follow-on). lzvn/lzfse/lzbitmap and resource-fork read are documented follow-ons; HFS+ already carries the lzvn/lzfse codecs.
scenario: format-image a 64 MiB SAKA5 container; commit-image-file-insert --compress-zlib stores a 20292-byte text payload (doc.txt) as an inline zlib com.apple.decmpfs attribute (no data blocks). The S.A.K. reader decodes it back byte-perfect; apfsck validates the structure; the macOS kernel mounts the synthesized container, decodes the file, and Apple fsck_apfs verifies container + volume.

evidence_fsck.host_result: apfsck clean (exit 0; ALLOCDBG fs_alloc_count=5 walked=5) -- apfsck reached and passed parse_decmpfs + apfs_compress_open (the inode is UF_COMPRESSED and the inline zlib inflates to exactly the header's uncompressed_size). Negative control: flipping one byte (block-zero) -> 'Block zero: bad checksum', exit 1.

evidence_unit: tests/unit/test_partition_manager_core.cpp: apfsWriter_insertsInlineCompressedFile (format -> compress-zlib insert of a >4096-byte payload -> reader byte-match round trip == original; listing size_bytes == uncompressed size; truncated read returns the requested prefix; container nx checksum intact + xid advanced; incompressible 8 KiB payload fails closed with the embedded-xattr-limit blocker). Full local core suite green (143/143); clang-format / lizard (CCN<=10, params<=5) / cppcheck (exhaustive) all clean.
kernel_mount: CERTIFIED 2026-06-27 in a macOS Sequoia VM (qemu/KVM recovery, apfs_kext 2332.101.1). The S.A.K.-written image (sha256 A20009A8...DB7D3FD) was attached on SATA (bare APFS container = /dev/disk0). The macOS kernel SYNTHESIZED the container (disk1, physical store disk0) and auto-mounted the volume at /Volumes/SAKA5; `md5 /Volumes/SAKA5/doc.txt` returned 829d6768845e56d8075ac7759ea1de01 -- BYTE-IDENTICAL to the host payload md5 (the kernel decoded the inline-zlib com.apple.decmpfs attribute to the exact original 20292 bytes). Then Apple's NATIVE fsck_apfs -n /dev/rdisk1: 'The volume SAKA5 was formatted by S.A.K. Utility APFS writer and last modified by apfs_kext (2332.101.1)', '** Checking the fsroot tree.', '** Checking the extent ref tree.', 'The volume /dev/rdisk1s1 ... appears to be OK.', 'The container /dev/rdisk1 appears to be OK.' Evidence apfs.a5-kernel-mount-md5-bytematch.png + apfs.a5-apple-fsck-PASS.png + apfs.a5-diskutil-list.png. The A5 lane flips to Yes.
